channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Washington Post editorial warns of potential Ukraine abandonment under Trump
item.description: The Washington Post’s editorial board released an op-ed on Saturday detailing the potential threats to Ukraine and the U.S. if President-elect Trump moves to strike a deal with his Russian counterparts. “The reason for this surge in fighting is clear: Mr.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Israeli strikes in central Beirut kill at least 20
item.description: BEIRUT, Lebanon (AP) — Israeli airstrikes Saturday in central Beirut killed at least 20 people, officials said, as the once-rare attacks on the heart of Lebanon's capital continued without warning while diplomats scrambled to broker a cease-fire.
item.dc:creator: ABBY SEWELL and BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israeli-Moldovan rabbi living in UAE is missing. Israeli officials fear he may have been kidnapped
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — An Israeli-Moldovan rabbi living in the United Arab Emirates has gone missing, with Israeli authorities raising the suspicion Saturday he may have been kidnapped as tensions remain high with Iran.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL and ELEANOR H. REICH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Deadly alcohol poisoning casts shadow over the Laotian backpacker town
item.description: VANG VIENG, Laos (AP) — A little town known as a backpacker paradise in northern Laos has come under spotlight for a mass poisoning case that has killed at least six tourists and potentially injured scores of others.
item.dc:creator: JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Philippine VP publicly threatens to have president assassinated
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — Philippine Vice President Sara Duterte said Saturday she has contracted an assassin to kill the president, his wife, and the House of Representatives speaker if she herself is killed, in a brazen public threat that she warned was not a joke.
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Modi's party heads for victory in Maharashtra state election while opposition wins Jharkhand
item.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s Hindu nationalist party is headed for a victory in state elections in politically significant Maharashtra while the opposition won mineral-rich Jharkhand state.
item.dc:creator: Ashok Sharma, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Fighting between armed sectarian groups in restive northwestern Pakistan kills at least 37 people
item.description: A senior Pakistani police officer says fighting between armed sectarian groups in the country's restive northwest has killed at least 37 people.
item.dc:creator: Riaz Khan, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: As fast fashion's waste pollutes Africa's environment, designers in Ghana are finding a solution
item.description: In a sprawling secondhand clothing market in Ghana’s capital, early morning shoppers jostle as they search through piles of garments, eager to pluck a bargain or a designer find from the stalls selling used apparel from the West.
item.dc:creator: Francis Kokutse, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Israel ambassador nominee Mike Huckabee: Hostages must be released
item.description: Israel ambassador nominee Mike Huckabee criticized the U.S. on Friday for pressuring Israel instead of holding Hamas accountable.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation Staff, NewsNation

item.title: A look inside the most expensive house in the world — the home of the UK’s monarch
item.description: Buckingham Palace in London is worth about $4.9 billion and has about 775 rooms, including royal and guest rooms, offices and bathrooms.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Prominent figure from Canada's trucker protests against COVID-19 restrictions found guilty
item.description: OTTAWA, Ontario (AP) — One of the most prominent figures from Canada's trucker protests against COVID-19 restrictions in 2022 has been found guilty on five counts including mischief and disobeying a court order.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Bahamas will refinance part of its debt to protect its famous turquoise waters
item.description: SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico (AP) — The Bahamas announced Friday that it will refinance $300 million of its external debt to free up more than $120 million for marine conservation projects and climate change mitigation.
item.dc:creator: DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Police blow up suspicious package outside US Embassy in London
item.description: Law enforcement conducted a “controlled explosion” of a suspicious package outside of the U.S. Embassy in London on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Pro-Ukraine lawmakers make last-ditch effort to safeguard US support
item.description: Pro-Ukraine lawmakers in both parties are working to get key provisions surrounding U.S. support for Kyiv passed into law by January, an effort to safeguard assistance that is under threat in a transition to a Trump administration and full GOP control of Congress.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, NewsNation

item.title: Hungary's Orbán vows to disregard international arrest warrant for Netanyahu
item.description: BUDAPEST, Hungary (AP) — Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orbán on Friday denounced the International Criminal Court’s issuing of an arrest warrant for Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, saying he would defy it by inviting him to Hungary.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Emperor penguin who swam to Australia released 20 days after waddling onto beach
item.description: The only emperor penguin known to have swum from Antarctica to Australia was released at sea 20 days after he waddled ashore on a popular tourist beach
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Death toll in Gaza from Israel-Hamas war passes 44,000, Palestinian officials say
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — The death toll in the Gaza Strip from the 13-month-old war between Israel and Hamas has surpassed 44,000, local health officials said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and FATMA KHALED, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pro-Ukraine lawmakers make last-ditch effort to safeguard US support
item.description: Pro-Ukraine lawmakers in both parties are working to get key provisions surrounding U.S. support for Kyiv passed into law by January, an effort to safeguard assistance that is under threat in a transition to a Trump administration and full GOP control of Congress.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: US citizen among 4 dead in Laos after suspected alcohol poisoning
item.description: Four people have died after drinking tainted alcohol in Laos after officials said that multiple people had been sickened in town popular with backpackers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ukrainians' approval of Zelensky, government at lowest point of war: Survey
item.description: Ukrainians' approval of President Volodymyr Zelensky and their national government has dropped more than 15 percentage points since 2023, a new Gallup survey revealed.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, The Hill

item.title: Treasury imposes sanctions on Russian banks, financial officials
item.description: Russia’s third-largest bank, Gazprombank, and its subsidiaries were hit with U.S. sanctions Thursday, making it the largest bank not already targeted as the Russia-Ukraine war nears the three-year mark.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Archaeologists discover 12,000-year-old pebbles that could provide new insights about the wheel
item.description: Researchers gave a detailed look into perforated stones collected out of excavations in Israel, and came to the conclusion that they could have served as spindle whorls.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope Francis rejects tradition, opts for humble wooden casket and burial outside Vatican
item.description: Pope Francis announced this week that he intends on having a much more simple funeral than those of his predecessors at the Vatican, including a humble wooden casket.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US vetoes UN resolution demanding a cease-fire in Gaza because there's no link to a hostage release
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — The United States on Wednesday vetoed a U.N. Security Council resolution demanding an immediate cease-fire in the war in Gaza because it was not linked to an immediate release of hostages taken captive by Hamas militants in Israel in October 2023.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: After 97 UN aid trucks in Gaza are ‘violently looted’ by armed men, food prices soar
item.description: Food prices are rising in Gaza following the robbery of a U.N. convoy over the weekend, during which, drivers were forced to unload aid at gunpoint, officials say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Leader of Georgia's separatist region of Abkhazia resigns, ending unrest
item.description: Following days of unrest in which the opposition seized government buildings, the leader of Georgia's separatist region of Abkhazia has resigned.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: World's hottest calendar drops 2025 edition, proceeds going to Hurricane Helene, Milton efforts
item.description: This year, the funds collected from calendar sales will support those impacted by Hurricanes Helene and Milton, to provide immediate relief and aid to impacted communities.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Bolton: Moscow, Beijing should feel ‘a lot of relief’ if Gabbard is confirmed as DNI
item.description: Former national security adviser John Bolton said that Russia and China should feel “a lot of relief” if former Rep. Tulsi Gabbard (Hawaii) is confirmed as President-elect Trump’s choice for director of national intelligence. “She’s not capable of doing it,” Bolton said Sunday on CNN.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Russia launches one of its fiercest missile and drone attacks at Ukraine's infrastructure
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — A Russian strike on a nine-story building in the city of Sumy in northern Ukraine killed eight people and wounded dozens, an official said Sunday, as Russia launched a massive drone and missile attack described by officials as the largest in recent months.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB and JOANNA KOZLOWSKA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Cruise line offers 4-year cruise to 'skip forward,' in wake of U.S. election results
item.description: One cruise line is offering travelers a customized journey for up to four years, to "skip forward," in the wake of the recent presidential election.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Wreckage of World War II warship found 80 years later
item.description: The USS Edsall, a World War II American warship, was bombed and sank three months after the attack on Pearl Harbor.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation
